# t22.1 - Aggregate completeness checkpoints and final conformance inputs

## Summary

Reissued the completeness input package so the conformance rerun can consume authoritative aggregate artifacts instead of the truncated checkpoint from `t22`. The implementation checkpoint now covers every plan-mapped delivery task plus the current remediation/review/runtime evidence, and the final traceability inputs are synchronized with the active PM/runtime findings.

## Deliverables

- [migration-summary.md](./migration-summary.md) - regenerated completeness summary with the current blocking conformance findings
- [checkpoints/tasks-to-impl.yaml](./checkpoints/tasks-to-impl.yaml) - authoritative aggregate implementation checkpoint for `t9` through `t20`
- [checkpoints/traceability-matrix.yaml](./checkpoints/traceability-matrix.yaml) - requirement-level traceability synchronized to PM/runtime evidence status

## Verdict

- Package integrity: PASS
- Conformance state: FAIL
- Critical findings carried forward: 3
- High findings carried forward: 1

## Notes

- The previous `tasks-to-impl.yaml` defect is repaired: the file is now structurally complete and carries an explicit aggregate validation block.
- The package remains red by design because `t21` parity gaps and the missing container-backed infra-tier evidence from `t6`/`t20` are still unresolved.
- `t22.2` should consume this package after `t20.1` and `t21.1` publish their remediation outputs.
